subject: English
title: A Question of Trust – Hard SNS

key_points: The story explores moral self-deception through Horace Danby’s character
Horace’s intelligence is weakened by emotional and ethical flaws
Selective morality allows Horace to justify crime without guilt
Trust functions as Horace’s tragic flaw
The young lady represents crime without conscience
Psychological manipulation proves stronger than physical force
Irony dominates the narrative, especially the ending
Poetic justice is delivered through Horace’s imprisonment
The story asserts that intelligence without ethics leads to downfall
characters: Horace Danby – A tragic figure undone by trust and hypocrisy
The Young Lady – Symbol of ruthless intelligence and amorality
Sherry – Represents deceptive calmness masking danger
important_events: Horace rationalises crime as morally acceptable
The young lady psychologically dominates the situation
Fear of prison strips Horace of control
The safe opening becomes the irreversible mistake
The ending delivers poetic justice through irony
exam_tips: Use terms like ‘selective morality’ and ‘tragic flaw’ in answers
Analyse characters instead of narrating the story
Explain how irony reinforces the moral message
Compare intelligence with wisdom where required
Relate the theme of trust to Horace’s downfall clearly
